description: Nuitka is a Python compiler written in Python. It translates Python code into C, and then compiles the C code into a native binary.

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/nuitka && python3 nuitka.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/nuitka && python3 nuitka.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/nuitka && python3 nuitka.py --help`. Python compiler (Python -> C -> native).
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the utility category. Fits when the engagement needs: Python compiler (Python -> C -> native).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Python compiler (Python -> C -> native).

name: nuitka

trust.notes: Operator-curated Python library. The pip name is resolved from PyPI; the import name is verified against the registry.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
